system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

{
    version     2.0;

// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    // Vertices 0-7
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (-50  25 -25)   // 1
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25  25)   // 3

    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - upper arm of bend
    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25  -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25  -50  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - lower arm of bend
    ( 25 -75  25)   // 12
    ( 25 -75 -25)   // 13
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 15

    (-500  -75  25)   // 16
    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section
    //   x=-50 to x=0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25
    // Face 0-3-2-1 at x=-50, face 4-7-6-5 at x=0
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper arm of bend
    //   x=0 to x=25, y=-25..0, z=-25..25
    // Face 4-5-9-8 at x=0, face 7-6-10-11 at x=25
    hex (4 5 9 8 7 6 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Lower arm of bend
    //   x=25, y=-50..-75, z=-25..25
    // Face 8-9-13-12 at x=25, but we need to extend in y
    // Actually this is a bit tricky - let's make it a proper block
    // y goes from -50 to -75, so face at y=-50 is 8-9-13-12
    // and face at y=-75 would be ... but we only have one layer in y
    // Let's treat this as a block that goes from y=-50 to y=-75
    // but our vertices only go down to y=-125... wait no, Block 2 uses 12,13,14,15 at y=-75,-125
    // Let me reconsider the block layout
    hex (8 9 13 12 11 10 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet straight section
    //   x=25 to x=-500, y=-125..-75, z=-25..25
    // This is a long thin block. Need to be careful with ordering.
    // Face at x=25: 12-13-14-15, face at x=-500: 16-17-18-19
    hex (12 13 17 16 15 14 18 19) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 19 18 17)
        );
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 9 8)
            (8 9 13 12)
        );
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (2 6 7 3)
            (14 18 19 15)
        );
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (0 3 2 1)
            (6 10 11 7)
            (10 14 17 16)
        );
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            (4 5 6 7)
            (12 13 17 16)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors (Persistent Across All Attempts)

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: Multiple blocks have issues with vertex ordering ("inside-out", "inward-pointing faces")
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading error)

## Root Cause

After 5 attempts, the fundamental problem is that the **vertex coordinates and block topology are fundamentally incompatible** with forming valid convex hexahedral blocks. The geometry spans widely varying extents in y and z directions across blocks, making it impossible to construct proper hex blocks with the current vertex assignments.

The critical issue revealed in Attempt 5 is that **vertices 12-15 are all at x=25** and **vertices 16-19 are all at x=-500**, but the outlet section needs to connect these over a massive x-distance while only spanning y=-125..-75 and z=-25..25. This creates an extremely elongated block with poor quality.

## Breakthrough Insight

The geometry needs to be redesigned so that **no block has vertices collapsed to a single plane or with extreme aspect ratios**. The solution is to **restructure the blockMeshDict to use a different vertex layout** where blocks share compatible face geometries.

## Proposed Solution

**Rewrite the entire `blockMeshDict`** with a physically meaningful and mathematically valid block topology. The key principles:

1. **Define vertices such that each hex block has vertices at 8 distinct corners of a proper parallelepiped**
2. **Ensure adjacent blocks share full faces (not partial edges or vertices)**
3. **Keep all blocks with reasonable aspect ratios**

Here is the corrected blockMeshDict structure:

```
vertices
(
    // Inlet face (x = -50)
    (-50,  25,  25)   // 0
    (-50,  25, -25)   // 1
    (-50, -25, -25)   // 2
    (-50, -25,  25)   // 3

    // Mid-plane above bend (y = 0, x = 0..25)
    (  0,  25,  25)   // 4
    (  0,  25, -25)   // 5
    (  0, -25, -25)   // 6
    (  0, -25,  25)   // 7

    // Bend corner (x = 25, y = 0)
    ( 25,  25,  25)   // 8
    ( 25,  25, -25)   // 9
    ( 25, -25, -25)   // 10
    ( 25, -25,  25)   // 11

    // Lower extension of bend (y = -50)
    ( 25, -50,  25)   // 12
    ( 25, -50, -25)   // 13
    (-500, -50,  25)  // 14
    (-500, -50, -25)  // 15

    // Outlet face (x = -500, y = -75..-125)
    (-500, -75,  25)  // 16
    (-500, -75, -25)  // 17
    (-500, -125, -25) // 18
    (-500, -125,  25) // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight section (x=-50 to x=0, y=-25..25, z=-25..25)
    hex (0 1 2 3 4 5 6 7) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Upper arm of bend (x=0 to x=25, y=-25..0, z=-25..25)
    hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Lower arm of bend (x=25, y=-50..-25, z=-25..25)
    hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Outlet section (x=25 to x=-500, y=-75..-50, z=-25..25)
    hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces ((0 3 2 1));
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces ((16 19 18 17));
    }

    upperWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces ((4 5 6 7) (8 9 10 11));
    }

    lowerWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces ((2 6 7 3) (12 13 14 15));
    }

    leftWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces ((0 4 8 12) (1 5 9 13));
    }

    rightWall
    {
        type wall;
        faces ((3 7 11 15) (2 10 14 18));
    }
);
```

## Key Changes

1. **Rebuilt blockMeshDict from scratch** with 5 vertices groups ensuring each hex block is a proper convex parallelepiped
2. **Fixed vertex ordering** in all hex blocks to follow right-hand rule consistently
3. **Updated boundary face definitions** to match the new block topology
4. **Removed problematic vertices** (like duplicating x=25 vertices that couldn't connect properly to x=-500)

## Error 2 Resolution

Once `blockMesh` succeeds, the mesh files (`points`, `faces`, `cells`) will appear in `constant/polyMesh/`, resolving the `log.rhoSimpleFoam` "cannot find points" error automatically.

## Post-Mesh Generation

After successful meshing, verify that all `0/` field files use the correct patch names: `inlet`, `outlet`, `upperWall`, `lowerWall`, `leftWall`, `rightWall`. No changes should be needed there since the boundary names match.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace entire file with corrected blockMeshDict; Define 20 vertices: 0=(-50,25,25), 1=(-50,25,-25), 2=(-50,-25,-25), 3=(-50,-25,25), 4=(0,25,25), 5=(0,25,-25), 6=(0,-25,-25), 7=(0,-25,25), 8=(25,25,25), 9=(25,25,-25), 10=(25,-25,-25), 11=(25,-25,25), 12=(25,-50,25), 13=(25,-50,-25), 14=(-500,-50,25), 15=(-500,-50,-25), 16=(-500,-75,25), 17=(-500,-75,-25), 18=(-500,-125,-25), 19=(-500,-125,25); Define 4 blocks: Block 0 hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for inlet section, Block 1 hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for upper bend, Block 2 hex (8 9 10 11 12 13 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for lower bend, Block 3 hex (12 13 14 15 16 17 18 19) (100 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) for outlet section; Set boundary patches: inlet with face (0 3 2 1), outlet with face (16 19 18 17), upperWall with faces (4 5 6 7) and (8 9 10 11), lowerWall with faces (2 6 7 3) and (12 13 14 15), leftWall with faces (0 4 8 12) and (1 5 9 13), rightWall with faces (3 7 11 15) and (2 10 14 18)'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 1.0.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
